course.title: Build a Production-Grade Pokedex in TypeScript
course.description: TypeScript learners ready to integrate strict runtime boundaries, a Node command lifecycle, Fetch, async state, fair-use caching, cancellation, persistence, security, testing, packaging, and operations into one cumulative learning product. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate a cumulative accessible typescript and node pokedex command product using documented pokéapi pagination, pokemon, and location-area contracts through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate unknown json validation, strict view models, fetch and abortsignal ownership, immutable async state, fair-use caching, promise deduplication, persistence, and recovery through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate original deterministic browser models plus controlled typescript 7, node 24, emitted-runtime, readline, network, filesystem, package, handle, load, and release transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five distinct stakeholder projects and a cumulative performance defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

credential.title: Build a Production-Grade Pokedex in TypeScript Certificate

modules.title: Learning Product Outcomes, Command Vocabulary, and Session Contract
modules.description: A science museum wants a Pokedex kiosk command but has not defined its learner decision, audience needs, command vocabulary, session boundaries, or evidence of understanding. Learners cumulatively produce bounded learner-facing command product charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define learner population, science or coding decision, command journey, accepted failure, feedback, and observable learning value.
Specify commands, arguments, examples, aliases, case and whitespace policy, invalid forms, and stable results as a small language.
Model ready, prompting, parsing, dispatching, waiting, reporting, aborted, closing, and closed states with legal transitions.

modules.title: Repository Baseline, Node 24, TypeScript 7, Compatibility, and Reproduction
modules.description: The prototype floats Node and TypeScript versions, confuses native TypeScript 7 CLI support with TypeScript 6 programmatic API compatibility, relies on generated residue, and lacks clean-install evidence. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible strict TypeScript Pokedex repository through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inspect revision, worktree, package metadata, lockfile, configuration inheritance, tests, fixtures, secrets, licenses, and recovery anchor.
Record supported Node 24 release, module system, platform, built-in fetch and readline surfaces, signal behavior, and package-manager contract.
Use TypeScript 7 CLI deliberately while retaining the documented TypeScript 6 compatibility package where programmatic tooling still requires it.

modules.title: Readline Promises, Stream Injection, EOF, Abort, and Close Ownership
modules.description: The loop creates multiple interfaces, reads global process streams inside domain code, ignores EOF and rejection, leaves question promises pending, and closes streams it does not own. Learners cumulatively produce injected abortable readline session adapter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Create one readline promises interface from injected input and output and distinguish interface ownership from stream ownership.
Pass an AbortSignal to pending questions, classify abort separately, and remove or one-shot listeners without leaks.
Handle EOF, close, error, quit, and repeated close idempotently while preserving an explicit final session outcome.

modules.title: Runtime Tokenization, Argument Validation, Registry Types, and Exhaustive Dispatch
modules.description: The parser splits on literal spaces, casts the first token to a command union, lets missing arguments become undefined, and uses an untyped object whose prototype can affect lookup. Learners cumulatively produce runtime-validated exhaustive command registry through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Normalize line endings and allowed whitespace under a bounded documented grammar while retaining raw input only when needed.
Narrow arbitrary strings against an own-key command registry instead of asserting they are CommandName.
Validate cardinality, emptiness, length, syntax, and command-specific arguments before constructing trusted handler input.

modules.title: Generated Help, Structured Results, Output Channels, and Accessibility
modules.description: Help and dispatch drift apart, handlers print directly, object iteration changes display order, errors use color alone, and progress rewrites one terminal line that assistive technology cannot follow. Learners cumulatively produce accessible deterministic terminal view layer through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Generate overview and command help from registry metadata with usage, examples, defaults, invalid forms, and close instructions.
Return a discriminated command result for data, usage, recoverable error, quit, and aborted outcomes before presentation.
Separate structured data, human status, diagnostics, and exit behavior through injected writers and stable formatting.

modules.title: PokéAPI Resources, URL API, Pagination Shapes, and Fair-Use Policy
modules.description: User input becomes a full fetch destination, URL strings are concatenated, pagination fields are assumed present, and repeated commands bypass the API request to cache every resource. Learners cumulatively produce fixed-origin typed PokéAPI request policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Hold scheme and authority in trusted configuration and map commands only to enumerated PokéAPI resource paths.
Validate resource kind, numeric identifier or canonical constrained name, and preserve requested versus resolved identity.
Use URL and URLSearchParams for encoded path and limit or offset values while rejecting slash, control, oversized, and unexpected input.

modules.title: Unknown JSON, Runtime Validators, Type Guards, Errors, and Schema Evolution
modules.description: response.json is typed as a trusted Pokemon, validators check only top-level keys, arrays contain unchecked members, optional fields are confused with undefined, and casts silence drift. Learners cumulatively produce strict unknown-to-domain validation pipeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Treat parsed external JSON as unknown and prevent it from entering domain or presentation code before validation.
Check non-null plain-enough objects, own keys, arrays, every member, primitive finiteness, bounds, and nesting explicitly.
Return a discriminated success or structured failure with path, expected shape, observed class, and bounded safe detail.

modules.title: Fetch, Response Admission, Abort Signals, Time Budgets, and Error Causes
modules.description: The client calls fetch globally, assumes only rejection means failure, parses every status as Pokemon, creates timeout controllers that outlive requests, and loses abort causes. Learners cumulatively produce injected bounded abort-aware Fetch client through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inject a narrow fetch-compatible port and keep Request, Response, and validation policy separate from domain commands.
Classify ok, not found, other 4xx, 5xx, redirects, media type, and unexpected response before parsing domain JSON.
Compose caller cancellation with bounded timeout using AbortSignal APIs and preserve the first meaningful reason.

modules.title: Pagination View Models, Immutable State, Map Transitions, and Stale Results
modules.description: The map command mutates offset before validation, exposes transport results to the UI, allows an older promise to overwrite a newer page, and assumes previous means offset minus limit. Learners cumulatively produce immutable stale-safe Pokemon page state through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Convert validated transport pages into readonly resource, cursor, result identity, count, and source metadata.
Derive candidate navigation and commit only a validated successful page while preserving prior state on failure.
Assign operation identity or generation so late responses cannot overwrite current state after newer commands.

modules.title: Navigation History, Immutable Snapshots, Back, and Bounded Undo
modules.description: History stores mutable references, pushes attempts before success, back triggers new hidden network work, unlimited pages accumulate, and undo can resurrect a stale pending request. Learners cumulatively produce bounded immutable navigation timeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define back as prior admitted view, not arithmetic or browser history, and expose when no snapshot exists.
Store readonly copied domain snapshots or stable cache identities without shared mutable arrays or pending controllers.
Push only after success, restore without hidden traffic when promised, and cancel or supersede pending operations safely.

modules.title: Cache Identity, TTL, Clock Injection, Readonly Copies, and Capacity
modules.description: The cache keys raw input, reads Date.now internally, shares mutable objects, conflates stale with absent, stores aborted promises, and has no entry or byte ceiling. Learners cumulatively produce observable bounded TypeScript resource cache through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Create a canonical typed key from endpoint, resolved identity, page parameters, and representation rather than user spelling.
Inject a clock and define fresh, stale, expired, and boundary instants without timer sleeps in unit tests.
Use immutable domain values, controlled cloning, or copy-on-read so consumers cannot rewrite cached or historical data.

modules.title: In-Flight Promise Deduplication, Waiters, Abort Ownership, and Cleanup
modules.description: Concurrent misses create duplicate fetches, a shared promise retains one caller signal, first cancellation rejects all waiters, and settled entries remain in the in-flight map. Learners cumulatively produce waiter-aware duplicate-suppressed async loader through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use the canonical resource key to register exactly one controlled load separate from the completed-value cache.
Give the loader its own controller and define waiter registration, completion, publication, and final cleanup.
Allow a waiter to stop observing without necessarily aborting work required by others and cancel shared work only under policy.

modules.title: Pokemon Detail Validation, Derived Types, Units, and Inclusive Presentation
modules.description: The inspect command trusts a cast response, renders raw API JSON, treats unit conversion as formatting, assumes arrays are nonempty, and hides which fields were unavailable. Learners cumulatively produce validated inclusive Pokemon detail card through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Validate numeric ID or constrained canonical name and retain both requested input and resolved resource identity.
Validate ID, name, types, abilities, stats, height, weight, sprites or references, bounds, and member shapes from unknown.
Map transport fields into a readonly discriminated view that separates available, empty, and unsupported information.

modules.title: Catch Policy, Injected Randomness, Immutable Collection State, and Updates
modules.description: Catch calls Math.random inside the handler, mutates a Map in shared state, stores transport objects, accepts duplicate names with different casing, and cannot replay outcomes. Learners cumulatively produce replayable immutable Pokemon collection reducer through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define what catch teaches, whether chance is justified, its visible probability contract, and duplicate and failure behavior.
Inject a bounded random-number port or deterministic policy and record draw evidence needed to replay a decision.
Return a new collection state and typed event for caught, missed, duplicate, failed, or aborted transitions without partial mutation.

modules.title: Owned Inspection, Stable Listing, Release Preview, and Accessible Export
modules.description: Owned inspect fetches remotely when absent, list order follows Map insertion accidents, release mutates before confirmation, and export dumps internal JSON without a version or accessibility structure. Learners cumulatively produce offline collection manager and structured export through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep owned inspection offline and return a typed not-owned outcome with explicit next commands.
Sort by documented ID or name policy, label totals and empty state, and retain stable output across reloads.
Construct an immutable preview, validate confirmation against exact identity, then return a new state and release event.

modules.title: Location-Area Validation, Encounter Relations, Filters, and Provenance
modules.description: The explore command confuses location endpoints, casts nested encounter arrays, filters before validation, collapses duplicate relationships, and produces an unbounded result list. Learners cumulatively produce validated provenance-rich encounter explorer through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish region, location, and location-area endpoints and choose the relation that answers the stakeholder question.
Validate area identity and every nested Pokemon, version detail, method, condition, chance, and level shape needed by the view.
Deduplicate only chosen display rows while retaining source area, version, method, chance, conditions, and original identity.

modules.title: Error Classes, Discriminated Results, Causes, Retry, and Session Recovery
modules.description: Catch blocks treat unknown as Error without narrowing, HTTP and validation failures share prose, retry wraps non-idempotent state transitions, causes are lost, and any rejection closes the session. Learners cumulatively produce causal typed recovery decision model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Treat caught values as unknown and normalize Error, DOMException, abort reasons, and non-Error throws at one boundary.
Represent input, not-found, HTTP, network, timeout, abort, validation, invariant, storage, and internal failures as exhaustive variants.
Retain safe causal chains and operation identity while separating internal diagnostics from user-facing recovery text.

modules.title: Process Signals, Abort Trees, Listener Cleanup, and Graceful Shutdown
modules.description: Every request owns an unrelated controller, SIGINT calls process.exit immediately, event listeners accumulate, pending readline and fetch work survive close, and aborted work prints as a defect. Learners cumulatively produce composed abort-aware Node session lifecycle through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Translate approved process signals into one root AbortController in the executable shell and define first and repeated signal behavior.
Compose session, command, timeout, and waiter signals while preserving reason and avoiding controller ownership confusion.
Use once or signal-bound listeners, remove handlers on completion, and prove no listener-count growth across commands.

modules.title: Bounded Promise Concurrency, Queueing, Ordering, Backpressure, and Cancellation
modules.description: Promise.all launches one fetch per result, queues grow without limits, abort rejects the aggregate and hides partial evidence, completion order changes output, and background rejections escape. Learners cumulatively produce bounded abortable detail-prefetch scheduler through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Justify prefetch from learner latency and API fair-use evidence and identify views where it must remain disabled.
Set active, queued, byte, request, time, and retry budgets independent of result count.
Track queued, active, settled, canceled, and published tasks with exactly one owner for scheduling and cleanup.

modules.title: Unknown File Data, Versioned Schemas, Atomic Save, Migration, and Restore
modules.description: Load casts parsed JSON, saves mutable state directly to the final path, lacks a version discriminator, assumes rename behavior, and replaces valid memory before all records pass validation. Learners cumulatively produce versioned validated collection persistence adapter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep browser work on pure string or byte models and isolate Node filesystem effects behind injected asynchronous ports.
Define an explicit versioned JSON schema for metadata, records, provenance, and integrity with deterministic serialization.
Bound bytes, parse into unknown, validate own keys and every member, reject duplicates and future versions, then commit state.

modules.title: SSRF Defense, Prototype-Safe Data, Terminal Injection, Paths, and Resource Limits
modules.description: Redirects escape the fixed host, parsed objects are merged into state, remote text contains ANSI or bidi controls, save paths are arbitrary, and response or command sizes are unbounded. Learners cumulatively produce deny-by-default TypeScript trust-boundary policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Revalidate scheme, hostname, port, path class, and redirect count for every destination and reject user-controlled egress.
Use own-property checks, explicit field construction, safe records, and no blind assignment from unknown objects.
Normalize or visibly escape control, ANSI, bidi, and non-printing sequences from commands, API payloads, and files.

modules.title: Node Test Runner, Runtime Faults, Compile Rejections, Async Leaks, and CLI Tests
modules.description: Tests cast fixtures, mock global fetch across files, assert only snapshots, omit compile-negative cases, leave timers and listeners active, and never execute emitted JavaScript. Learners cumulatively produce layered runtime and compile-time evidence harness through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate pure validator and reducer, fetch, readline, cache, persistence, subprocess, compile-negative, fuzz-like, and controlled live smoke evidence.
Use node:test hooks, deterministic fixtures, scoped mocks, injected ports, fake clocks or deferred promises, and independent state.
Use original minimal unknown payloads that cover valid, changed, malformed, hostile, oversized, aborted, and reordered behavior.

modules.title: Causal Telemetry, Event-Loop Responsiveness, Latency, Cache Value, and Capacity
modules.description: Logs capture full payloads, metric labels contain Pokemon names and commands, average latency hides event-loop stalls, cache hit rate ignores workload, and optimization increases async fan-out. Learners cumulatively produce privacy-minimized interactive capacity report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Correlate command, operation generation, cache decision, request class, attempt, status class, duration, outcome, abort reason, and cleanup with bounded fields.
Count commands, outcomes, cache decisions, requests, bytes, aborts, queue depth, validation and persistence failures without unbounded labels.
Measure user-visible cold and warm percentiles plus event-loop delay under a declared scripted workload and environment.

modules.title: ESM Packaging, Exports, Executable Entry, Artifact Inspection, and Platform Support
modules.description: The command runs only through tsx in the source tree, package exports expose internals, the bin entry points at TypeScript, files include fixtures and secrets, and support claims ignore Node and OS behavior. Learners cumulatively produce installable inspected Node Pokedex package through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define package type, source and output roots, explicit extensions, imports, exports, internal boundaries, and supported Node resolution.
Publish an executable JavaScript entry with portable launcher behavior, thin process wiring, correct permissions, and no source-tree assumptions.
Use package files or ignore rules, inspect npm pack output, include license and docs, exclude secrets and residue, and verify dependency provenance.

modules.title: End-to-End Release, Migration, Recovery, and TypeScript Engineering Defense
modules.description: A polished candidate passes type checking and familiar tests but lacks emitted-runtime, abort, accessibility, fair-use, package, failure, migration, rollback, restore, and residual-risk proof. Learners cumulatively produce defensible recoverable TypeScript Pokedex release dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Bind source, lockfile, compiler, tests, emitted JavaScript, packed artifact, command behavior, saved data, checksums, and stakeholder review to one revision.
Execute invalid input, not found, HTTP failure, malformed JSON, stale Promise, abort, cache corruption, write interruption, signal, and listener-leak cases.
Prove schema migration, old-package compatibility limits, backup, rollback, restored data identity, and support decisions.
